rationale: Obligation requires proving r1.id == r2.id where id: Vec<u8>, given only r1@ == parse_arg_as_end_point(arg@) and r2@ == parse_arg_as_end_point(arg@). The antecedent yields r1.id@ == arg@ == r2.id@ (Seq<u8> equality, provable). In this Verus version (vstd at /home/chentianyu/nanvix/toolchain/verus/vstd), Vec is declared with #[verifier::external_type_specification] + #[verifier::external_body] and its view() is `uninterp spec fn view(&self) -> Seq<T>` (vstd/view.rs:104). There is NO axiom asserting view is injective, Vec is not Structural, and there is no #[verifier::ext_equal] on Vec. The closest lemmas (vstd/std_specs/vec.rs: lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq, lemma_vec_obeys_deep_eq) only relate `eq_spec` (PartialEqSpec) to view equality, NOT the spec-level `==`. A minimal repro `proof fn(v1: Vec<u8>, v2: Vec<u8>) requires v1@ == v2@ ensures v1 == v2 {}` fails to verify, confirming the obligation is unsatisfiable without introducing an axiom (assume/assume_specification/external_body), all of
